attempted_route: sklearn.metrics.pairwise.cosine_similarity between flattened W .npy matrix (1152-dim) and count-based multi-hot vocab vector built from model CSV 'Prediction' column (9-dim)
observation: ValueError: Incompatible dimension for X and Y matrices: X.shape[1] == 9 while Y.shape[1] == 1152 at cosine_similarity call; exit_code=1 after 1s
rationale: The agent built a 9-element response-vocabulary count vector from the CSV but tried to compare it with a 1152-element flattened W matrix. The W matrix encodes JNMF latent factors x syllogisms, not a flat response-vocab vector, so dimensions are irreconcilable without projection or reshaping to the same space.
recommended_alternative: Load each model CSV as a numeric matrix matching the W shape (rows=syllogisms, cols=responses); compute numpy.dot(model_matrix.flatten(), W_pattern.flatten()) / (numpy.linalg.norm(model_matrix.flatten()) * numpy.linalg.norm(W_pattern.flatten())) for cosine similarity, ensuring both arrays are flattened to the same total element count (1152) before comparison.
